channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Dara Khosrowshahi on replacing Uber drivers — and himself — with AI
item.description: Today, I’m talking with Uber CEO Dara Khosrowshahi. It’s become something of an annual tradition to have Dara join us in the studio when he comes to New York for Uber’s big GO-GET event every...

item.title: Tesla hits Musk’s threshold for ‘safe unsupervised’ driving
item.description: We've crossed yet another one of Elon Musk's self-driving thresholds. Tesla's fleet of vehicles using the company's Full Self-Driving (Supervised) system has driven over 10 billion miles,...

item.title: Your First Autonomous Agent Mesh – Easier Than You Think
item.description: In this guide we’ll build a Decentralized, Autonomous Vacation Booking System in Python using the Protolink library. The original post can be found on medium (Level-up-coding). The landscape of AI...

item.title: Building AI Agents with Spring AI and Amazon Bedrock AgentCore - Part 2 Deploy Conference Search application on AgentCore Runtime
item.description: Introduction In the article Introduction to Spring AI, we introduced the sample application to search for conferences. We also exposed its functionality as a set of MCP-compatible tools. In the...

item.title: CLMA Frame Test

item.title: From Glue to Horizon: Our Real Journey Building an Iceberg Lakehouse on Snowflake
item.description: We set out to build an open lakehouse: Iceberg tables on AWS S3, Spark/Glue for pipelines, Snowflake for analytics compute power. What could go wrong? Everything—from query performance implosions...

item.title: Stop Using Regex for Invoices: Use AI to Extract Line-Items in Seconds
item.description: The Nightmare of Parsing Invoices If you’ve ever tried to extract structured data from an invoice or receipt, you know exactly how painful it is. You write a perfect regular expression to extract...

item.title: The 27-Question Client Intake Form Every Small Agency Actually Needs
item.description: Most agency onboarding fails before the kickoff call happens. Not because the team isn't good. Not because the client is difficult. Because nobody collected the right context upfront, and the...

item.title: What are your goals for the week? #177
item.description: Friday I got a new camera to replace the one that broke last year. It's a Canon Rebel with two lenses. The buttons and functions are different from the previous camera so my muscle memory is no...

item.title: Hyped and Overhyped Programming Languages in 2026
item.description: Every few years the industry rediscovers that programming languages are not religions. Then we immediately behave like they are religions. Someone posts a benchmark. Someone else says memory...

item.title: AI Has Hands Now. You Should Decide What It Can Do.
item.description: We’ve crossed a line. AI is no longer just generating text. It’s starting to take actions. Your agent can now: call APIs delete data send emails trigger workflows modify production systems And in...

item.title: I Built a Chrome Extension in 5 Months as a Teenager: Here’s My CometNotes Story
item.description: “I’m 11, I don’t have a CS degree, Here’s how I built an extension with AI” The Problem I had a problem. Every time I had an idea, I’d think: “I should write that down.” By the time I opened...

item.title: GameStop makes $56 billion offer to acquire eBay
item.description: GameStop has made an offer to acquire eBay for $56 billion, which comes as part of GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen's plan to transform the online marketplace into a "legit competitor to Amazon," he tells...

item.title: Tailoring AI solutions for health care needs
item.description: The AI market is full of big promises of grand transformation. Health care is a prime target for those promises, beset as it is by financial pressures, labor shortages, and the growing burden of...

item.title: Hisense aggressively cuts the price of its RGB LED TV on release day
item.description: The Hisense UR9 - the first RGB LED TV to be released this year - is now available for much less than originally revealed. The 65-inch UR9 is now $1,999, while the 75-inch model is $2,999 and the...

item.title: Homebridge 2.0 is here, and it speaks Matter
item.description: Homebridge, the popular software solution that allows you to integrate non-HomeKit devices like Ring cameras into Apple Home, is adding support for the Matter smart home standard. Today, May 4th,...

item.title: 2026: The Year of AI-Assisted Attacks
item.description: On December 4, 2025, a 17-year-old was arrested in Osaka under Japan’s Unauthorized Access Prohibition Act. The young man had run malicious code to extract the personal data of over 7 million...

item.title: Silver Fox Deploys ABCDoor Malware via Tax-Themed Phishing in India and Russia
item.description: The China-based cybercrime group known as Silver Fox has been linked to a new campaign targeting organizations in Russia and India with a new malware called ABCDoor. The activity involved using...

item.title: Hacking Polymarket
item.description: Polymarket is a platform where people can bet on real-world events, political and otherwise. Leaving the ethical considerations of this aside (for one, it facilitates assassination), one of the...

item.title: Critical cPanel Vulnerability Weaponized to Target Government and MSP Networks
item.description: A previously unknown threat actor has been observed targeting government and military entities in Southeast Asia, alongside a smaller cluster of managed service providers (MSPs) and hosting...

item.title: Global Crackdown Arrests 276, Shuts 9 Crypto Scam Centers, Seizes $701M
item.description: A coordinated international operation involving U.S. and Chinese authorities has arrested at least 276 suspects and shut down nine scam centers used for cryptocurrency investment fraud schemes...

item.title: ‘This is fine’ creator says AI startup stole his art
item.description: The ad comes from Artisan, the AI startup behind billboards urging businesses to "stop hiring humans."

item.title: In Harvard study, AI offered more accurate emergency room diagnoses than two human doctors
item.description: A new study examines how large language models perform in a variety of medical contexts, including real emergency room cases — where at least one model seemed to be more accurate than human doctors.

item.title: Reggie Fils-Aimé says Amazon once asked Nintendo to break the law
item.description: Way back in the DS days, Nintendo decided to stop selling to Amazon. During a recent lecture at NYU, former Nintendo of America President Reggie Fils-Aimé said it was because Amazon was seeking...

item.title: Shokz’s bassy OpenRun Pro 2 are $40 off thanks to a new Mother’s Day promo
item.description: If you’re looking to pick up a pair of open-ear headphones for yourself — or your mom — Shokz is running a Mother’s Day sale. Now through May 10th, the company’s best pair of bone conduction...

item.title: These reusable digital Polaroids are a clever way to cover a fridge in memories
item.description: I love when my friends have their fridges covered in Polaroids. It’s often a visual crash course of what’s been going on in their lives chronicling recent adventures, get-togethers, and other fun...

item.title: How the internet’s favorite squirrel dad made the hottest camera app of 2026
item.description: It's not hyperbole to call DualShot Recorder an overnight sensation. It took only 12 hours from the time it was released to hit number one on the App Store's list of top paid apps. It was a...

item.title: AI music is flooding streaming services — but who wants it?
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on how AI is changing music and the music industry, follow Terrence O'Brien. The Stepback...

item.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited Linux Root Access Bug CVE-2026-31431 to KEV
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added a recently disclosed security flaw impacting various Linux distributions to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities...

item.title: AI-generated actors and scripts are now ineligible for Oscars
item.description: Bad news for Tilly Norwood.
